semanticCore: Intestines, bowels, guts, digestive tract, or inside hollow

keyDerivations.meaning: Intestine, bowel, gut, or digestive canal

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَسُقُوا مَاءً حَمِيمًا فَقَطَّعَ أَمْعَاءَهُمْ (47:15): And they are given to drink scalding water, so it tears/cuts their intestines/bowels.
discardedNoise: Ma'a (with/alongside) - preposition particle (unrelated to this root).
summaryEn: The root refers to internal intestines (Mi'a), describing the horrific physical destruction of Hell's inhabitants whose bowels are severed by drinking boiling water.
summaryAr: تشير مادة هذا الجذر إلى المعى والأمعاء وهي مجاري الطعام والشراب داخل البطن الأحشاء (معي)، واصفاً تقطيع أمعاء أهل النار بشربهم الماء الحميم المغلي.
rawText: الأَمْعَاءُ i. q. الأَقْصَابُ; (AO: see voce قِتْبٌ;) the guts; i. e. bowels, or intestines, into which the food passes from the stomach: الحَشَا is the name of all the places of the food; and in the belly are the أَعْفَاج and the أَقْتَاب, to which the food passes after the stomach, and these are the lower امعاء; and all these are called the قُصْب: the حَوَايَا are all the امعاء that wind, or take a coiled, or circular, form. (Zj, in his “ Khalk el-Insán. ”) === أَمْعَآءُ: see a tropical signification (water-holes) of this pl. voce حَوِيَّةُ. مِعًى A narrow, depressed place (See طَأْطَاءٌ.) === بَنَاتٌ مِعًى The بَعْر. (T in art. بنى.) === المِعَى المُسْتَقِيمُ The rectum.
